title: Data Sources
description: Data Sources represent the OMOP CDM databases registered within Parthenon. This section provides tools to configure, profile, and monitor connected data sources, including uploading raw data, managing ETL processes, and mapping source concepts to standard vocabulary.
tips: Profile each new data source using the Source Profiler before running analyses to understand its clinical coverage and volume.
Validate concept mapping completeness for key clinical domains—unmapped source codes can significantly affect cohort capture rates.
Monitor ingestion job status in Jobs Monitoring to ensure data loads complete successfully before scheduling analyses.
Document the CDM version and vocabulary release associated with each data source to support study reproducibility.
Use FHIR Ingestion tooling when integrating data from FHIR-conformant source systems to streamline the transformation process.
